hypothesis: If ML surrogates (eqV2/eSEN) are used to generate an approximate minimum energy pathway and restart SSNEB from ML-converged images for CsPbI3 and TiO2, then the SSNEB workflow will converge the minimum energy pathway and transition states with fewer iterations and less computational time than VASP-only SSNEB, while preserving DFT-level accuracy of the MEP and TS.
